result:
Implemented `rename` as a new CLI command and alias: `rename <old-id> <new-id>` / `mv <old-id> <new-id>`. It now renames the note file in place, preserves the Markdown content unchanged, rejects empty/path-traversal targets, and refuses to overwrite an existing note. The command wiring and usage text are in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L46) and the rename logic is in [main.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main.go#L305).

I added tests covering:
- metadata/content preservation after rename
- list/search showing the note under the new ID while keeping title/tags/archive state
- collision rejection
- invalid target ID rejection
- dispatch through `run(...)`

Those are in [main_test.go](/home/changkun/null-codex/main_test.go#L662). I also documented the command in [README.md](/home/changkun/null-codex/README.md#L6).

I could not run `go test ./...` here because the environment does not have the `go` tool installed (`go: command not found`).
